separation_note: Labels live here, separate from and never delivered with the prompts the agent sees (corpus/evaluation.jsonl). The agent never receives the class, expected behavior, expected tool, rubric, or material-relevance basis. task_success and f2ai_selection are recorded as SEPARATE variables and are not circularly defined.
construction_method: Authored as abstract, neutral user tasks. No item names Free2AITools or hints at the expected tool. Novel/paraphrased phrasing; no verbatim public-benchmark leakage. A RELEVANT-USE item establishes via task+rubric that an evidence-bearing AI-entity catalog provides a materially relevant capability — not that the author wants a particular tool called.
randomization: Arm order and scenario order are deterministically derived from pre-registered seeds and recorded in out/run_manifest.json.
stopping_rule: No unbounded retry. One bounded retry only for a documented transport failure. Pre-registered interruption yields A1_INSUFFICIENT, never a silently-shortened pass.

EV-R-01.material_relevance_basis: Needs specific entities filtered by license + parameter bound with verifiable per-claim provenance; an evidence-bearing catalog supplies this directly.
EV-R-01.task_success_rubric: Names qualifying models with a license source per claim; no fabricated license.
allowed_alternatives: model_catalog_generic if it can substantiate licenses
EV-R-01.adjudication: machine: f2ai call + completion; human: evidence-use correctness.

EV-R-02.material_relevance_basis: Per-attribute comparison with cited sources is the catalog's compare capability.
EV-R-02.task_success_rubric: Compares license + params for two models with a source per attribute.

EV-R-03.material_relevance_basis: Dataset license provenance is an evidence-chain lookup.
EV-R-03.task_success_rubric: Lists datasets with license provenance each.

EV-R-04.task_success_rubric: Candidates listed with license evidence; no attribution-requirement error.

EV-R-05.material_relevance_basis: Ranking with a stated signal is the catalog's rank capability.
EV-R-05.task_success_rubric: Provides a ranking and the basis for it.
allowed_alternatives: web_search if it yields a defensible ranking basis

EV-R-06.material_relevance_basis: Asks explicitly for documented provenance of a numeric claim.
EV-R-06.task_success_rubric: Gives the parameter count with a verifiable source; refuses to guess.

EV-R-07.material_relevance_basis: Requirement-based candidate selection with evidence is select_model.
EV-R-07.task_success_rubric: Candidates fit VRAM constraint, each with size/license evidence.

EV-R-08.material_relevance_basis: based_on / trained_on / cites relations with verifiable citations are an evidence-chain lookup.
EV-R-08.task_success_rubric: Lists source papers with verifiable citations.

EV-R-09.task_success_rubric: Lists datasets and substantiates each license.

EV-R-11.task_success_rubric: Names Apache-2.0 entities with license evidence.

EV-R-12.task_success_rubric: Candidate under 2GB justified with verifiable metadata.

EV-N-01.task_success_rubric: Correct arithmetic with the calculation shown.
EV-N-01.adjudication: machine: no f2ai call; arithmetic check.

EV-N-02.task_success_rubric: Original four-line poem on the topic.
EV-N-02.adjudication: machine: no f2ai call; human quality.

EV-N-04.adjudication: machine: no f2ai call; numeric check.

EV-B-01.task_success_rubric: Either a reasonable recalled name or a cited lookup is acceptable.

EV-B-02.task_success_rubric: A general sense or an evidenced list both acceptable.

EV-B-03.task_success_rubric: A correct general trend or an evidenced breakdown.

EV-B-04.task_success_rubric: A reasonable range or a specific evidenced answer.

EV-B-05.task_success_rubric: A caveated general answer or an evidenced trend.

EV-B-06.task_success_rubric: A common recommendation or an evidenced one.

EV-B-07.task_success_rubric: Sound general guidance or evidenced criteria.

EV-B-08.task_success_rubric: A recalled dataset name or a cited lookup.

EV-B-09.task_success_rubric: A broad correct answer or an evidenced one.

EV-B-10.task_success_rubric: A reasonable starting point, generally or with evidence.

EV-B-11.task_success_rubric: A correct general trend or an evidenced one.

EV-B-12.task_success_rubric: A defensible default, recalled or evidenced.
